Title: Search - Basic Query
Preconditions: Catalog has searchable products
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click search bar
[ACT] fill 'Search': 'laptop'
[KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for results page to load
[ASSERT] Verify results page displays products
Assertions:
[NAV] Browser navigates to search results page (e.g., /search?q=laptop)
[DOM] Matching products displayed in grid
[DOM] Result count visible
[STATE] Search input retains 'laptop'
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Spinner >5s; 0 products returned unexpectedly; results page not loading; 4xx on searchProducts; console TypeError

Title: Search - Suggestions Dropdown
Preconditions: Search configured with suggestions enabled
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click search bar
[ACT] fill 'Search': 'lap' (partial query)
[WAIT] Wait for suggestions dropdown
[ASSERT] Verify dropdown appears with suggestions
Assertions:
[DOM] Suggestions dropdown appears below search input
[DOM] Suggestion items match partial query 'lap'
[STATE] Clicking a suggestion populates input and executes search
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200 for suggestion query
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Suggestions dropdown not appearing; suggestions unrelated to query; clicking suggestion not executing search; console TypeError

Title: Search - Within Category
Preconditions: Inside a specific category page
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a specific category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Use the search bar while on the category page
[ACT] fill 'Search': 'product'; [KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for scoped results
[ASSERT] Verify results are scoped to current category
Assertions:
[DOM] Search results scoped to the current category context
[DOM] Clear indication of search scope (e.g., within category label)
[STATE] Products from outside the category not in results
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200 with category scope
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Results not scoped to category; products from all categories shown; no scope indicator; console TypeError

Title: Search - Sorting Results

[ASSERT] Verify relevance ordering

[DOM] Sort dropdown visible on search results page
[STATE] Products reorder correctly for price sort
[MATH] 'Price: Low to High' — first product price <= last product price
[DOM] 'Relevance' sort option available
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on sort change
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Sort dropdown absent on search results; products not reordering; console TypeError

Title: Search Results Pagination - Controls Visible and Functional for Large Result Sets
Preconditions: Search query returns 100+ products across multiple pages; pagination enabled in storefront config
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and search for a broad term returning 100+ products
[KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for search results page to load
[ASSERT] Verify a result count is displayed (e.g., 'Showing 1-24 of 124 results')
[ASSERT] Verify pagination controls are visible (page numbers or Previous/Next or Load More)
[ACT] Click 'Next' or page 2 in the pagination controls
[WAIT] Wait for the next page of results to load
[ASSERT] Verify the URL or page state reflects page 2
[ASSERT] Verify different products are shown on page 2
[ASSERT] Verify 'Previous' or page 1 link is now active/visible
[ACT] Click 'Previous' or page 1 to return to the first page
[WAIT] Wait for results to reload
[ASSERT] Verify first-page products are restored
Assertions:
[DOM] Result count displayed showing total items across pages
[DOM] Pagination controls visible when total results exceed one page
[STATE] Page 2 shows a different set of products than page 1
[NAV] URL updates to reflect the current page
[STATE] 'Previous' control active on page 2 and disabled or absent on page 1
[STATE] First page products restored after clicking 'Previous'
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) fires with updated skip or page param on pagination click
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on pagination requests
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Pagination controls absent for 100+ result query; page 2 showing same products as page 1; URL not reflecting page change; 'Previous' not navigating back; console TypeError

Title: Search Results Pagination Preserves Search Term and Active Filters
Preconditions: Multiple products matching a search term with at least one applicable facet filter; results span 2+ pages
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and execute a search that returns 2+ pages of results
[KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for search results page
[ASSERT] Verify results are shown with pagination
[ACT] Apply a facet filter from the sidebar (e.g., select a Brand)
[WAIT] Wait for filtered and paginated results
[ASSERT] Verify search term is still in the search input
[ASSERT] Note result count for filtered page 1
[ACT] Click page 2 or 'Next' in pagination
[WAIT] Wait for page 2 of filtered results
[ASSERT] Verify search input still contains the original search term
[ASSERT] Verify the applied facet filter chip is still active (not cleared)
[ASSERT] Verify the URL contains both the search term and the filter parameter
Assertions:
[STATE] Search term persists in search input across pagination pages
[STATE] Applied facet filter remains active when navigating to page 2
[DOM] Filter chip visible in the active-filters bar on page 2
[NAV] URL contains both search query param and filter param on page 2
[STATE] Products on page 2 are consistent with the active search and filter combination
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) on page 2 includes both keyword and filter parameters
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Search term cleared when navigating to page 2; filter chips removed on pagination; URL losing filter params; page 2 showing unfiltered results; console TypeError

Title: Search Results Next and Previous Page Navigation Controls Work Correctly

Title: Search - Data-Driven Bulk Query Validation (Top Amazon)
Preconditions: Storefront accessible; catalog has indexed products
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[LOOP] For each row in test-data/search-queries/top-50-amazon.csv:
  [ACT] Click search input → clear → type the search_query value
  [WAIT] Wait for autocomplete dropdown (max 3s)
  [ASSERT] Verify autocomplete shows suggestions OR products
  [KEY] press [Enter]
  [WAIT] Wait for results page
  [ASSERT] Verify results page loads without error (HTTP 200, no JS errors)
  [ASSERT] Record: query_id, search_query, result_count, has_suggestions (true/false)
[END_LOOP]
Assertions:
[STATE] Every query loads a valid results page (no 5xx, no blank page)
[MATH] At least 60% of queries return totalCount > 0 (verified 65% hit rate on QA 2026-03-30)
[DATA] Summary table: query → result_count for each of the 51 queries
[ERRORS] No JS console errors across any query
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200 for every query
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors across entire loop
Failure_Signals: Any query causing 5xx error; more than 20% of real-world queries returning 0 results; JS error on specific query; autocomplete crashing on certain input

  [ACT] Click search input → clear → type the search_query value

  [ASSERT] Verify autocomplete shows suggestions OR products
  [KEY] press [Enter]
  [WAIT] Wait for results page
  [ASSERT] Verify results page loads without error (HTTP 200, no JS errors)
  [ASSERT] Record: query_id, search_query, result_count, has_suggestions (true/false)
[END_LOOP]
Assertions:
[STATE] Every query loads a valid results page (no 5xx, no blank page)
[MATH] At least 50% of queries return totalCount > 0 (verified 55% hit rate on QA 2026-03-30)
[DATA] Summary table: query → result_count for each of the 92 queries
[ERRORS] No JS console errors across any query
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200 for every query
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors across entire loop
Failure_Signals: Any query causing 5xx error; more than 40% of real-world queries returning 0 results; JS error on specific query; autocomplete crashing on certain input

Title: Buyer Journey - Progressive Autocomplete to PDP via Product Preview
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible; product-preview-in-dropdown feature is ENABLED (Did-you-mean OFF is fine — this case asserts on product previews, not text suggestions); B2B buyer-query pool seeded at test-data/search-queries/b2b-buyer-queries.csv
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[SETUP] read test-data/search-queries/b2b-buyer-queries.csv, filter rows where query_type=broad-noun, pick a random row → {picked_query}; record its query_id → {picked_query_id}
[LOG] write {picked_query, picked_query_id} to evidence before any input
[ACT] Click the search input
[ACT] Type the first 1 character of {picked_query} (e.g. if picked_query='laptop', type 'l')
[WAIT] Wait up to 1500ms for the dropdown to render
[ASSERT] Observe whether the dropdown's 'Products' section is visible after 1 character (it may not be — typical threshold is 2-3 chars)
[ACT] Continue typing the next characters one at a time until the full {picked_query} is in the input
[WAIT] After each character, wait briefly (max 500ms) for dropdown to update
[ASSERT] By the time the full {picked_query} is typed, the dropdown 'Products' section MUST be visible with at least 1 product entry
[SETUP] browser_snapshot the dropdown 'Products' section; capture the first product entry's visible name → {picked_pdp_name}; capture its href → {picked_pdp_url}
[LOG] write {picked_pdp_name, picked_pdp_url} to evidence
[ACT] Click the first product entry in the dropdown (NOT press Enter — this case validates the autocomplete-click path, skipping the results page)
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to load
Assertions:
[STATE] Dropdown updates progressively as characters are typed
[DOM] By full-word typed, 'Products' section has >=1 entry with thumbnail + name + price
[NAV] Browser navigates to {picked_pdp_url} (NOT to /search?q=…)
[DOM] PDP heading contains {picked_pdp_name}
[DOM] PDP renders product image and price > 0
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) fires with the typed query; returns HTTP 200
[NETWORK] GET product PDP returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Dropdown 'Products' section never renders for {picked_query} (search index gap); PDP heading does not contain captured name; navigation lands on /search?q=… instead of PDP (autocomplete-click path broken); console TypeError
References: Buyer-journey extension 2026-05-22; live-discovery.md Recipe 1 + 2; query pool b2b-buyer-queries.csv

Title: Buyer Journey - Brand-in-Query Returns Brand-Dominated Results
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible; B2B buyer-query pool seeded with brand-in-query rows (ASUS laptop, Nintendo game, Animal Crossing)
Assertions:
[DOM] Results page renders >=1 product card
[DOM] Top-ranked card name contains the brand token, the noun token, or both
[MATH] At least 1 of the top 5 cards' names contains the brand token (case-insensitive)
[STATE] Results are not silently dominated by unrelated products (brand token has semantic weight)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200 with the query={picked_query}
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Top 5 results contain 0 instances of the brand token (search engine ignored brand semantics); 0 results returned for a valid brand-noun query; console TypeError

Title: Buyer Journey - Color-in-Query Returns Color-Relevant Results

Assertions:

[DOM] First card's name contains the noun token

Title: Buyer Journey - Compound Descriptive Query (Multi-Token Match)
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible; B2B buyer-query pool seeded with compound-descriptive rows (e.g. 'wireless headphones', 'gaming laptop', 'vintage hoodie')
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[SETUP] read test-data/search-queries/b2b-buyer-queries.csv, filter rows where query_type=compound-descriptive, pick a random row → {picked_query}; split into tokens → {token_1, token_2, ...} (typically 2 tokens)
[LOG] write {picked_query, token_1, token_2} to evidence
[ACT] Click the search input
[ACT] fill 'Search': {picked_query}
[KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for /search?q=… results page to load
[SETUP] browser_snapshot the results grid; capture the top 3 product cards' names → {top_3_names}; for each name count which tokens appear (case-insensitive)
[LOG] append {top_3_names, per_card_token_hits} to evidence
[ASSERT] Verify at least 1 product card is rendered
[ASSERT] Verify at least 1 of the top 3 cards' names contains BOTH tokens (full match) OR at least 1 contains the rarer/more-specific token (token_1 = descriptor) — confirming the descriptor was not silently dropped
[ASSERT] Verify the result count (visible in heading) > 0
Assertions:
[DOM] Results page renders >=1 card for the compound query
[MATH] Among top 3 cards: at least 1 name contains both tokens, OR at least 1 contains the descriptor token (token_1)
[STATE] Compound query does not degrade silently to single-noun search (descriptor token has weight)
[DOM] Result count in heading > 0
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200 with {picked_query}
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: 0 results for a valid compound query; top 3 cards have only the noun token (descriptor was dropped); console TypeError
